stem:
The equation ax = b, where a and b are constants, has no solutions. Which of the following must be true?

I. a = 0

II. b = 0

III. b ≠ 0
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The equation ax = b has no solutions exactly when the left side is identically 0 while the right side is not, i.e., when a = 0 and b ≠ 0. So I and III must hold, but II is the negation of III.

Choices A and D each capture only one of the two required conditions. Choice C contradicts III.
